items.title: feat(api): return Retry-After header from the rate limiter
items.body:
Implements ACME-204.

When a client exceeds its rate limit we now return `Retry-After: <seconds>` alongside the 429, computed from the token bucket refill time. Enterprise plans also get `X-RateLimit-Remaining` on every response.

Testing: added `test_rate_limiter_retry_after.py`; verified on staging with `curl -i https://staging.acmetech.io/api/v1/orders` in a loop until 429.

comments.body: Approach looks right. One thing: clamp Retry-After to 3600 so a misconfigured bucket can't tell clients to wait a week.

Second approval. Always squash-merge. Never merge without 2 approvals unless it's a hotfix with the 'hotfix'/'urgent' label. Never force-push over review comments — append commits, squash at merge. First review pass within 24 hours. Keep PRs under ~400 lines.

Squash-merged. Deploying per the runbook: staging is green, promoting with `make deploy ENV=prod` after the canary bake. Our deployment process: merge to main -> CI runs -> auto-deploy to staging -> manual promote to prod with `make deploy ENV=prod`. Smoke tests run first, then 5% canary, 30-min bake, then full rollout (ACME-247). Watch the error dashboard for 10 minutes after. Rollback: `make deploy ENV=prod VERSION=<previous>`. No Friday deploys.

items.title: feat(status): auto-update status page from monitoring alerts
items.body:
Implements ACME-209 (requested by support in #incidents).

When an alert with severity P0/P1 fires in Alertmanager, we now POST to the Statuspage API (`POST /v1/pages/{page_id}/incidents`) to open an investigating incident automatically. Closes when the alert resolves. This gets us inside the 15-minute status page SLA from the Incident Response Playbook even when the on-call is heads down mitigating.

comments.body: Love it. Make sure the Statuspage token comes from vault, not env — same pattern as the pager token.
2nd approval, squash away.

items.title: feat(webhooks): mandatory signature verification on inbound integrations
items.body:
Implements ACME-217 (security ask from Sarah).

All inbound webhooks must now carry `X-Acme-Signature: sha256=<hmac>` computed over the raw body with the per-integration secret. Verification: constant-time compare, reject with 401 on mismatch, 30-day dual-accept window for integrations still migrating (flag `webhooks.require_signature`).

comments.body: This closes the top finding from the pentest. Approval #1.
Approval #2. Verified the constant-time compare uses `hmac.compare_digest`, not `==`.

items.title: feat(dashboard): rate limit usage dashboard (phase 1)
items.body:
Implements ACME-254 (Dan's epic, phase 1 = visibility only).

New admin panel page showing per-account rate limit consumption, with a callout for legacy plan accounts whose configured limit differs from the plan default (the ACME-228 class of confusion).

comments.body: Screenshot matches the spec, ship it. (Not an approval — PM approvals don't count toward the 2, per the review guidelines.)
Approval #1. Traced the query, looks right.
Approval #2. Squash-merge when CI is green.

items.title: feat(errors): show request id in the error page footer
items.body:
Implements ACME-263. My first PR here!

Error pages now render `request_id` in the footer so support can paste it straight into the log search. Format matches what `X-Request-Id` returns on API responses.

comments.body: Welcome aboard! Requested one change: truncate the id to the first 8 chars visually but copy the full id on click — support pastes these into Kibana.

Approval #2. FYI since this is your first merge: Our deployment process: merge to main -> CI runs -> auto-deploy to staging -> manual promote to prod with `make deploy ENV=prod`. Smoke tests run first, then 5% canary, 30-min bake, then full rollout (ACME-247). Watch the error dashboard for 10 minutes after. Rollback: `make deploy ENV=prod VERSION=<previous>`. No Friday deploys.
Merged — and per ACME-227 this unlocks your prod read access. Ask Priya to provision it.

items.title: feat(exports): in-app progress + direct browser download for data export
items.body:
Implements ACME-273.

The 'export my data' flow now shows an in-app progress indicator and triggers the download in the browser when the async job completes. The email link stays as a fallback (it still lands in junk for ~30% of Outlook enterprise seats, see ACME-234).

comments.body: Support will throw a party. This is our #2 ticket driver.
Approval #1 — backend polling endpoint is clean.
Approval #2, squash-merge.

items.title: feat(alerts): page on-call when DB pool saturation crosses 80%
items.body:
Implements ACME-236, action item from the June 9 P0 postmortem (ACME-201 / issue #136).

New Prometheus alert `DBPoolSaturationHigh`: fires at 80% pool usage for 5 minutes, pages the on-call primary per the paging policy (P1 during business hours). At 95% it escalates to P0.

comments.body: This would have given us 20 minutes of warning on June 9. Approval #1.
Approval #2. Postmortem action items get priority over feature work, glad this landed inside the week.

items.title: feat(dev): devcontainer-based development environment
items.body:
Implements ACME-224.

Adds `.devcontainer/` with the full stack (Postgres, Redis, MinIO). `make dev` brings everything up; onboarding doc updated. New joiners should be able to run the test suite on day 1 instead of day 3.

comments.body: Tested as the resident newest person: clean clone -> `make dev` -> green tests in 22 minutes. Approval #1.
Approval #2.

items.title: feat(changelog): public changelog RSS feed
items.body:
Implements ACME-262.

Serves `/changelog.rss` generated from the changelog markdown at build time. Dan wants enterprise customers to subscribe instead of emailing support for release notes.

comments.body: Validates in the W3C feed checker. Nice.
Approval #1.
Approval #2. Squash-merge.

items.title: feat(auth): SSO domain allowlist per enterprise account
items.body:
Follow-up to the Initech SSO escalation (ACME-208).

Enterprise accounts can now restrict SSO logins to an allowlist of email domains. Prevents the class of failure where a contractor with a personal address gets bounced with an opaque error — we now return a clear 'domain not allowed by your administrator' message.

comments.body: Initech and Globex both asked for exactly this in QBRs.
Approval #1.

Approval #2. Always squash-merge. Never merge without 2 approvals unless it's a hotfix with the 'hotfix'/'urgent' label. Never force-push over review comments — append commits, squash at merge. First review pass within 24 hours. Keep PRs under ~400 lines.

items.title: fix(billing): serialize refund webhook handling to stop double refunds
items.body:
Fixes ACME-221.

Root cause: when Stripe redelivers the same refund webhook within ~200ms, both handler invocations pass the 'already refunded?' check before either writes.

The fix: (1) `SELECT ... FOR UPDATE` on the charge row so concurrent handlers serialize, and (2) a unique index on `refunds.stripe_event_id` as a belt-and-braces idempotency guard — the second insert now fails cleanly with a no-op.

Repro test included: fires the same webhook twice concurrently with `asyncio.gather`, asserts exactly one refund row.

comments.body: Approval #1. The unique index is the important half — locks protect us only inside one process.

Approval #2. Money-touching fix, so: deploy to staging, run the billing smoke suite (`make smoke SUITE=billing`), then promote. Our deployment process: merge to main -> CI runs -> auto-deploy to staging -> manual promote to prod with `make deploy ENV=prod`. Smoke tests run first, then 5% canary, 30-min bake, then full rollout (ACME-247). Watch the error dashboard for 10 minutes after. Rollback: `make deploy ENV=prod VERSION=<previous>`. No Friday deploys.

items.title: fix(billing): correct rounding for annual plans with mid-cycle proration
items.body:
Fixes ACME-203.

We were rounding at each proration step (float), accumulating up to $0.04 error on 12-line invoices. Fix: compute in `Decimal` with `ROUND_HALF_EVEN`, round once at the invoice total. Backfilled test cases from the three customer invoices Tom flagged.

comments.body: Confirmed against the Initech invoice from the ticket — matches their finance team's number to the cent now.
Approval #1.
Approval #2, squash-merge.

items.title: fix(webhooks): exponential backoff with jitter for failing endpoints
items.body:
Fixes ACME-231.

The dispatcher retried failing endpoints every 10s forever, which is what amplified the June 9 outage (ACME-201). Now: exponential backoff 30s -> 1m -> 5m -> 30m -> 2h with +/-20% jitter, and a circuit breaker that disables an endpoint after 3 days of continuous failure (customer gets an email at disable time).

comments.body: Approval #1 — this is postmortem action item #2 from ACME-201. Retry storms were half the pool exhaustion.
Approval #2.

items.title: fix(webhooks): release DB connections on the dispatcher error path
items.body:
Fixes ACME-235 — the actual root cause of the June 9 P0 (ACME-201).

The dispatcher acquired a connection, then raised before the `release()` when the delivery POST failed. Under a retry storm that leaks the whole pool in ~20 minutes. Fix: connection acquisition moved into an `async with pool.acquire()` block so every path releases; added a leak-detection test that asserts pool.free == pool.size after 50 simulated failures.

comments.body: Approval #1. This plus #113 closes the two root causes from the postmortem (issue #136).
Approval #2. Deploy today please — we're one retry storm away from a repeat until this ships.

items.title: fix(frontend): Safari 18 renders blank dashboard charts
items.body:
Fixes ACME-215.

Safari 18 dropped support for the deprecated `OffscreenCanvas.convertToBlob` path our chart lib used behind a feature check that lies on Safari. Fix: pin the chart renderer to the 2D context path on WebKit, added a Playwright run on WebKit to CI so this can't regress silently.

comments.body: Two enterprise demos hit this last week, thanks for the quick turnaround.
Approval #1 — repro'd on Safari 18.1, fixed.
Approval #2.

items.title: fix(billing): idempotency key on charge creation for webhook retries
items.body:
Fixes ACME-266.

When the payment provider times out and retries the checkout confirmation webhook we created a second charge (Northwind got billed twice — see ACME-253 for the $12k refund fallout). Fix: charge creation now carries an idempotency key derived from `checkout_session_id`; the second webhook finds the existing charge and returns 200 without writing.

comments.body: Northwind's CFO will be pleased. Their renewal literally hinged on us fixing this class of bug.
Approval #1. Same pattern as #111 — idempotency at the write, not just a read-check.
Approval #2. Billing smoke suite on staging before promote, as always for money paths.

items.title: fix(ci): quarantine flaky test_partial_refund_rounding
items.body:
Fixes ACME-249.

The test seeded `random` for amounts but not for the proration date, so month-length differences flipped the expected rounding in ~3% of runs. Fix: freeze the date with `freezegun`, parametrize the month-boundary cases explicitly. 500 local runs, 0 failures.

comments.body: Approval — test-only change, so 1 approval suffices per the review guidelines. Thanks for killing the retry-until-green culture before it starts.

items.title: fix(invoices): correct 'recieved' typo in the invoice PDF template
items.body: Fixes ACME-268. Reported by Aisha after a customer screenshotted it, which is a special kind of embarrassing.

comments.body: Approval — docs-only, 1 approval is enough. Also grepped for other instances: this was the only one.

items.title: chore(deps): bump sqlalchemy from 2.0.30 to 2.0.35
items.body:
Bumps [sqlalchemy](https://github.com/sqlalchemy/sqlalchemy) from 2.0.30 to 2.0.35.

Release notes: bug fixes in the async session lifecycle and typing improvements. No breaking changes noted in the changelog.

comments.body: Patch-range bump, CI green including the billing smoke suite. Per our policy patch/minor merges once CI is green: @dependabot squash and merge

items.title: chore(deps): bump fastapi from 0.111.0 to 0.111.1
items.body:
Bumps [fastapi](https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi) from 0.111.0 to 0.111.1.

Patch release: dependency pin fix for starlette.

comments.body: CI green, patch bump. @dependabot squash and merge

items.title: chore(deps): bump react from 18.3.1 to 19.0.0
items.body:
Bumps [react](https://github.com/facebook/react) from 18.3.1 to 19.0.0.

Major version. Breaking changes: removed legacy context, new JSX transform required, ref-as-prop.

comments.body:
Major bump — our policy is majors wait for the quarterly dependency rotation (ACME-258) where we do them as planned work with real testing, not drive-by merges.

@dependabot ignore this major version
OK, I won't notify you about version 19.x.x again, unless you re-open this PR.

items.title: chore(deps-dev): bump pytest from 8.2.1 to 8.3.2
items.body:
Bumps [pytest](https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest) from 8.2.1 to 8.3.2.

Minor release: improved assertion rewriting, fixes for xdist interaction.

comments.body: Dev-only dep, CI green. @dependabot squash and merge

items.title: chore(deps): bump stripe from 9.8.0 to 9.12.0
items.body:
Bumps [stripe](https://github.com/stripe/stripe-python) from 9.8.0 to 9.12.0.

Adds new API surface for Payment Links; no breaking changes listed.

comments.body: Money-touching dependency, so CI green isn't enough: deployed the branch to staging and ran the billing smoke suite (`make smoke SUITE=billing`) against the Stripe test account first. Refund + proration flows all pass.
That's the right process for anything that touches billing. @dependabot squash and merge

items.title: chore(deps): bump jsonwebtoken from 9.0.1 to 9.0.2 (security)
items.body:
Bumps jsonwebtoken from 9.0.1 to 9.0.2.

**This update fixes a security vulnerability** (GHSA-xxxx: signature verification bypass under crafted algorithm headers).

comments.body:
Security patches merge same-day per policy — no waiting for the weekly deps pass. CI green. @dependabot squash and merge

Deploying to prod today, not waiting for the Thursday train.

items.title: chore(deps): bump moment from 2.30.1 to 2.30.2
items.body: Bumps [moment](https://github.com/moment/moment) from 2.30.1 to 2.30.2.

comments.body:
We're removing moment entirely this quarter (ACME-255 — it's 40% of the bundle). No point taking updates for a library on death row.

@dependabot ignore this dependency
OK, I won't notify you about moment again, unless you re-open this PR.

items.title: [HOTFIX] rollback webhook dispatcher to v2026.06.08 build
items.body:
Mitigation for the ongoing P0 (ACME-201): API error rate 40%, DB pool at 100/100. The dispatcher shipped yesterday isn't releasing connections on its error path; rolling back to the previous artifact while we fix forward.

For hotfixes: create branch from main, fix, get 1 approval, merge with the [HOTFIX] prefix and the 'hotfix' + 'urgent' labels, deploy immediately (skip the 30-min canary bake), then backport and file the follow-up review ticket within 24 hours.

comments.body: Approval — 1 is enough for a hotfix with the urgent label. Deploying immediately with `make deploy ENV=prod VERSION=2026.06.08`, skipping the canary bake per the hotfix process. War room is in #incidents.
Deployed 14:47. Pool usage dropped 80 -> 45 -> normal. Recovery declared 14:52. Follow-up review ticket filed (ACME-235), postmortem due within 48h per the playbook — tracking in issue #136.

items.title: [HOTFIX] keep previous JWKS keys valid for 24h after rotation
items.body:
Fixes ACME-233 (P0: auth service rejecting valid tokens after JWKS rotation).

Root cause: rotation dropped the old signing key instantly, so every token issued before rotation failed verification until expiry. Fix: keep the previous key in the verification set for 24h (one full token lifetime) after rotation.

For hotfixes: create branch from main, fix, get 1 approval, merge with the [HOTFIX] prefix and the 'hotfix' + 'urgent' labels, deploy immediately (skip the 30-min canary bake), then backport and file the follow-up review ticket within 24 hours.

comments.body: Approval (1 needed, hotfix). Merge and deploy now, backport to the release branch after. File the follow-up review ticket within 24h.
Deployed 09:05, token verification error rate back to baseline. Backport PR is #139-internal; follow-up review ticket ACME-233-followup filed. Postmortem in issue #137.

items.title: [HOTFIX] revert gateway config push + add schema validation
items.body:
Mitigation + fix for ACME-212 (P0: gateway rejecting all requests after a malformed config push).

Part 1 (deployed immediately): revert to the last-known-good config from the config repo history.
Part 2 (this PR): the gateway now validates config against a JSON schema at load and refuses to apply invalid config, keeping the previous one live. A bad push becomes a no-op with an alert instead of an outage.

comments.body: Approval — hotfix rules. Config revert already live (outage was 11:02-11:09). This PR is the fix-forward so it can't happen again.

Merged and deployed. For hotfixes: create branch from main, fix, get 1 approval, merge with the [HOTFIX] prefix and the 'hotfix' + 'urgent' labels, deploy immediately (skip the 30-min canary bake), then backport and file the follow-up review ticket within 24 hours.

items.title: [HOTFIX] per-account rate limit override for migrated enterprise plans
items.body:
Fixes ACME-270: Globex hard-blocked by the rate limiter after their plan migration reset them to the default tier limit.

Fix: plan migrations now carry over any account-level rate limit override, and support can set overrides from the admin panel (previously required a deploy). Globex unblocked via manual override at 10:12 while this merges.

comments.body: Globex confirms traffic is flowing. SLA credit conversation is tracked in ACME-265.
Approval (hotfix, 1 needed). Merge, deploy, backport. Follow-up review ticket within 24h please.

items.title: [HOTFIX] jittered retry + client backoff for S3 SlowDown responses
items.body:
Fixes ACME-245 (P1: file uploads failing with S3 503 SlowDown).

We hammered a single key prefix; S3 responded with 503 SlowDown and our client failed uploads immediately. Fix: retry SlowDown with exponential backoff + jitter (max 5 attempts), and shard the upload key prefix by account hash to spread the partition load.

comments.body: Approval — hotfix path: branch from main, 1 approval, deploy immediately, backport after. Upload success rate recovering on the dashboard already.

items.title: Postmortem: June 9 P0 — DB connection pool exhaustion, full API outage
items.body:
Postmortem for ACME-201, filed within 48h per the Incident Response Playbook. Blameless — we postmortem the system, not the person.

TIMELINE (UTC):
- 14:14 alert fires, Raj declares P0 in #incidents, war room open
- 14:16 on-call (Priya) paged per P0 policy
- 14:21 pool confirmed at 100/100, all requests queueing
- 14:28 webhook dispatcher (previous day's deploy) identified — not releasing connections on its error path
- 14:35 rollback initiated (PR #131)
- 14:47 rollback deployed, pool 80 -> 45 -> normal
- 14:52 recovery declared

ROOT CAUSE: connection leak on the dispatcher error path (ACME-235, fixed in #114), amplified by retry storm from failing webhook endpoints (ACME-231, fixed in #113).

CUSTOMER IMPACT: 38 minutes of elevated errors, peak 40%. Globex SLA credit request tracked in ACME-265.

WHAT WENT WELL: detection to war room in 2 minutes; rollback as mitigation (always acceptable per playbook) beat debugging live.

ACTION ITEMS (all carry the postmortem label, prioritized above feature work):
- [x] Fix connection leak (ACME-235 / #114) — Priya
- [x] Retry backoff + circuit breaker (ACME-231 / #113) — Lucy
- [x] Pool saturation alert at 80% (ACME-236 / #107) — Elena
- [ ] Load test the dispatcher error path in CI — Raj

comments.body: Reviewed at Thursday standup. All action items have owners; the open one is on next sprint. Closing.

items.title: Postmortem: June 12 P0 — auth service rejected valid tokens after JWKS rotation
items.body:
Postmortem for ACME-233.

TIMELINE (UTC):
- 08:02 scheduled JWKS rotation runs
- 08:04 token verification failures spike to 100% for pre-rotation tokens
- 08:07 P0 declared in #incidents (Aisha, from support volume)
- 08:20 root cause identified: old key dropped instantly
- 08:55 hotfix #132 merged (1 approval, hotfix path)
- 09:05 deployed, error rate baseline by 09:12

ROOT CAUSE: rotation removed the previous signing key immediately instead of keeping it for one token lifetime.

ACTION ITEMS:
- [x] 24h dual-key window (#132) — Priya
- [ ] Alert on token verification failure rate > 5% — Raj
- [ ] Rotation runbook page in Confluence — Priya

comments.body: Good writeup. Note support detected this before monitoring did — that's the gap the alert action item closes.

items.title: Postmortem: duplicate billing reminder emails to ~800 customers (lightweight)
items.body:
Lightweight postmortem for ACME-256 (P1, small blast radius — one pager, no meeting, per the playbook).

WHAT HAPPENED: the nightly billing-reminder cron ran twice — the k8s node hosting the job was recycled mid-run and the job was rescheduled without an idempotency guard. ~800 customers got duplicate emails.

FIX: reminder send now records `(customer_id, billing_date)` in a sent-log table with a unique constraint; the rescheduled run no-ops on conflict.

PATTERN: this is the third idempotency bug this month (see #111, #116). Anything that can run twice WILL run twice — writes need idempotency keys, not just read-checks.

comments.body: That pattern note is the valuable part. Adding 'idempotency review' to the code review guidelines for anything touching crons, webhooks, or retries.

items.title: Bug: password reset emails delayed 10-30 min or never arriving
items.body:
Mirrors ACME-210, filed here for the engineering fix.

REPRODUCTION:
1. Request a password reset for any account on prod
2. Watch the email queue dashboard
3. The reset email sits in `queue:transactional` behind bulk marketing sends

EXPECTED: transactional email within 60 seconds.
ACTUAL: 10-30 min at peak; SendGrid returns 429 on our shared IP pool and the retry requeues at the back.

Support impact: ~15 tickets/week, users think signup is broken (see #support threads from June 5).

comments.body: Fix plan: dedicated transactional queue with priority dispatch + separate SendGrid subuser so bulk sends can't starve resets. Tracked as ACME-210.

items.title: Bug: admin panel shows plan-default rate limit instead of account override
items.body:
Mirrors ACME-228.

REPRODUCTION:
1. Pick a legacy-plan account with a custom rate limit override (e.g. any pre-2025 enterprise account)
2. Open Admin > Account > Limits
3. Panel shows the plan default, not the effective override

IMPACT: support quotes wrong limits to customers (twice for Globex — related fallout in ACME-270 / #134).

comments.body: Fixed as part of the rate limit dashboard (#104) — the panel now reads the effective limit from the same endpoint the limiter uses. Single source of truth.

items.title: Bug: Storybook broken on main after theme tokens PR
items.body:
Mirrors ACME-240.

REPRODUCTION:
1. `git checkout main && npm run storybook`
2. Every story throws `undefined is not an object (theme.tokens.spacing)`

CAUSE: the theme tokens PR renamed `spacing` -> `space` but Storybook's decorator still injects the old shape. CI doesn't build Storybook so nothing caught it.

comments.body: Fixed the decorator and added `npm run storybook:build` to the CI path filter for frontend changes, so a broken Storybook now fails the PR instead of surprising the next person.

items.title: Bug: nightly build time regression 12 min -> 25 min after runner image bump
items.body:
Mirrors ACME-244.

REPRODUCTION: compare any nightly run before/after June 14 — the runner image bump to ubuntu-24.04 dropped the layer cache.

BISECTED: the new image invalidates the Docker layer cache because the base image digest changed; every nightly rebuilds all layers from scratch.

comments.body: Fixed: pinned the base image by digest and moved dependency layers before source layers so code changes don't bust the cache. Nightly is back to 13 min.

items.title: Feature request: automate the canary stage of the deployment runbook
items.body:
Follow-up to ACME-247 (runbook update: smoke tests, 5% canary, 30-min bake, rollback).

Today the canary promote is manual: deploy to staging, run smoke tests, promote 5% with `make deploy ENV=prod CANARY=5`, watch the error dashboard for the 30-min bake, then full rollout. The ask: automate the bake-watch so the pipeline auto-promotes when error rate and p99 stay inside thresholds, and auto-rolls-back otherwise (like the payments-service auto-rollback that saved us in ACME-226).

comments.body: Agreed. Thresholds proposal: error rate < 1% AND p99 < 800ms over the full bake window. Manual promote stays available as an override.
+1. The June 9 outage window would have been ~10 min shorter with auto-rollback on the dispatcher deploy.

items.title: Feature request: surface Retry-After in client SDKs
items.body:
Support-driven follow-up to #101 / ACME-204.

Now that the API returns Retry-After on 429s, the client SDKs should honor it automatically instead of hammering. Enterprise customers (Globex, post ACME-270) are asking for built-in backoff so their integrations degrade gracefully.

comments.body: Scheduling for next sprint. Python SDK first (largest install base), then Node.

items.title: Bug: data export completion emails flagged as spam for Outlook recipients
items.body:
Mirrors ACME-234.

REPRODUCTION:
1. Trigger 'export my data' with an @outlook.com / Microsoft-hosted recipient
2. Export-ready email lands in junk (~30% of enterprise seats)

CAUSE (working theory): missing List-Unsubscribe header + link shortener domain on the CTA button trips Microsoft's filter.

The real fix is making email unnecessary for the common path — in-app download shipped in #106. This tracks the deliverability fix for the fallback email.

comments.body: #106 removes most of the pain. For the email itself: drop the shortener, add List-Unsubscribe, and we're moving transactional sends to the dedicated subuser from #139 anyway.
